target.title: fix(update): continue after package doctor warnings
target.body:
## Summary

- Downgrade completed nonzero package post-install `openclaw doctor --fix` results to an advisory update step after the package install has already been verified.
- Keep killed/timed-out doctor runs fatal so interrupted repairs do not continue into plugin convergence or gateway restart.
- Surface advisory doctor stderr in both live progress and printed update summaries, then continue into the fresh post-core update process.
- Fix the progress renderer typings for advisory doctor summary rows so the PR head passes the prod/test type gates.

## Verification

- `pnpm format:fix src/cli/update-cli/progress.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/update-cli.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)
- Real package update proof from a packed branch tarball with a Node preload hook that forces only the post-install `doctor` child to exit nonzero while `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS=1` is set.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Package updates that successfully install and verify a new core package now continue past only an explicitly signaled recoverable post-install doctor result. Ordinary unknown nonzero doctor exits, killed runs, and timed-out runs remain blocking.

Real environment tested: Local macOS package install/update path on PR head `377924d03129cc97b094c1d2681148e37487e5f0` using a packed branch tarball, an isolated `npm install -g --prefix`, isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, and a real `openclaw update --yes --no-restart --tag <local-tarball> --json` invocation. The proof state used a real SQLite installed-plugin record for Discord with a deliberately missing payload path so the post-install doctor child wrote the typed advisory result itself during the update doctor pass.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
npm pack --pack-destination /tmp/openclaw-pr-91586-proof.vEmzlN/pack
npm install -g --prefix /tmp/openclaw-pr-91586-proof.vEmzlN/prefix /tmp/openclaw-pr-91586-proof.vEmzlN/pack/openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz
# Seed isolated state/openclaw.sqlite installed_plugin_index.install_records_json so discord.installPath=/missing/discord.
PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr-91586-proof.vEmzlN/prefix/bin:$PATH \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr-91586-proof.vEmzlN/state \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr-91586-proof.vEmzlN/state/openclaw.json \
  openclaw update --yes --no-restart --tag /tmp/openclaw-pr-91586-proof.vEmzlN/pack/openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz --json
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "mode": "npm",
  "doctor": {
    "name": "openclaw doctor",
    "exitCode": 86,
    "advisory": {
      "kind": "package-post-install-doctor",
      "message": "Post-install doctor reported a recoverable update-time repair warning after the package install was verified; continuing with post-core plugin convergence and gateway restart."
    },
    "stderrTail": "Config warnings:\n- plugins.entries.discord: plugin not installed: discord — install the official external plugin with: openclaw plugins install @openclaw/discord\nSkipped package-manager repair for configured plugin \"discord\" during package update; rerun \"openclaw doctor --fix\" after the update completes.\nPost-install doctor reported a recoverable update-time repair warning after the package install was verified; continuing with post-core plugin convergence and gateway restart."
  },
  "postUpdatePluginsStatus": "warning"
}
```

Observed result after fix: The real updater completed with `status: "ok"` after package install and install swap succeeded. The post-install doctor child returned the dedicated advisory exit code `86` with `advisory.kind: "package-post-install-doctor"`, and the update continued into post-core plugin convergence. The post-core plugin result was `warning` because the proof intentionally left Discord's payload missing to exercise the deferred repair path; it did not stop at the doctor step.

What was not tested: This local proof used `--no-restart`, so it did not exercise managed gateway restart in the same run. The prior dedicated Testbox Docker restart lane remained blocked before lane start by organization access: `You can only access resources in your authenticated organization.`
